caseDescription: The brass service key to the Aurora Grand's locked green room vanished after a midnight broadcast rehearsal and a private dressing ledger disappeared with it. Five staff members remained inside the velvet concourse wing, but only one suspect survives the full logic of the valid clue, the planted red herring, and the neutral backstage detail.
locationImagePromptBase: empty theatre concourse lineup setting inside a velvet-lit backstage corridor, polished floor with perspective lines, brass wall sconces, paneled walls, shallow steps and door frames giving depth, enough open floor for five standing suspects, no people, no characters
featuredImagePromptBase: midnight theatre service corridor after a break-in, empty brass key hook outside the green room, half-open ledger drawer, velvet glow from dressing mirrors, no people, physical clue atmosphere
instruction: Study the lineup, compare the interviews, and weigh each evidence role carefully: one clue is true, one clue is a trap, and one only helps the story make sense.
explanation: The guilty suspect is Elara Wynn, slot 5. The valid evidence is Stage Wax Seam, which places Elara's costume satchel at the green room threshold because the same green sealing wax from the missing ledger drawer is caught in her satchel seam. Brass Cuff Smear is the red herring: it points toward Rowan Voss because he handles brass lamp fittings, but that transfer comes from routine maintenance rather than the theft. Cue Sheet Pin Mark is neutral evidence; it proves the rehearsal order was changed and narrows the time window, but it does not accuse anyone by itself. Elara's access authority, the narrowed timeline, and the valid wax transfer make her the only suspect consistent with the full case.

suspects.dialogue: I closed the costume alcove after the lead's change. I never touched the green room drawer once rehearsal broke for the broadcast call.
suspects.profile: Age 43, exacting wardrobe mistress who treats garment handling like sacred ritual and notices the smallest backstage disorder.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide an unauthorized costume loan that would embarrass the house manager.
suspects.notes: Strong access to satchels and stage wax, but her station logs keep her inside wardrobe during the key window.

visualIdentity.silhouette: structured long smock with squared apron line
visualIdentity.wardrobe: dark tailoring smock, pinned measuring tape, fitted boots, cuffed sleeves
visualIdentity.hair: dark hair in a tight rolled bun
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: silver thimble chain at the waist
visualIdentity.props: garment ledger and pin cushion bracelet

suspects.portraitPromptBase: wardrobe mistress, structured long smock with squared apron line, fitted boots, silver thimble chain, garment ledger, pin cushion bracelet, forest green seam ribbon accent, upright exacting stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I was on the brass lamps along the concourse arch, not in the green room. If there's polish on my cuff, that's from the sconce housings.
suspects.profile: Age 39, practical house electrician who keeps the old theatre alive with cable runs, lamp polish, and improvised fixes.
suspects.motive: Could want to cover a prohibited wiring shortcut near the broadcast mirror bank.
suspects.notes: The red herring naturally points at him because he works around brass fixtures, but the stronger transfer chain does not reach his tool roll.

visualIdentity.silhouette: boxy electrician jacket with heavy side satchel
visualIdentity.wardrobe: utility jacket, rolled sleeves, dark work trousers, heavy lace-up boots
visualIdentity.hair: short sandy hair with rough sideburns
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: brass tester clipped to chest pocket
visualIdentity.props: coil of cable and leather tool satchel

suspects.portraitPromptBase: house electrician, boxy work jacket with heavy side satchel, rolled sleeves, lace-up boots, brass tester, coil of cable, leather tool satchel, amber stitching accent, open planted stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I rewrote the rehearsal order and sent everyone to marks. The green room remained locked while I called the mirror cue from the concourse desk.
suspects.profile: Age 34, controlled stage manager with a reputation for turning chaotic rehearsal nights into hard-edged precision.
suspects.motive: Could be tempted to bury a late cue change that violated the producer's approved running order.
suspects.notes: Her cue paperwork makes the timeline important, but that same paperwork becomes neutral context rather than guilt.

visualIdentity.silhouette: long-line blazer with clipped pencil skirt profile
visualIdentity.wardrobe: tailored blazer, slim gloves, pencil skirt, polished heels
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: headset loop resting at the collar

suspects.portraitPromptBase: broadcast stage manager, long-line blazer with clipped pencil skirt profile, slim gloves, polished heels, headset loop, cue clipboard, stopwatch cord, wine-red lapel lining accent, forward directing posture, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I stayed by the rehearsal piano after the last cue. I don't carry service keys, and I don't root through the green room drawer for ledgers.
suspects.profile: Age 36, smooth resident pianist who drifts easily between orchestra pit, rehearsal lounge, and private performer favors.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide a private booking note tucked inside the missing dressing ledger.
suspects.notes: He has social access but no direct link to the service key, satchel wax, or changed mirror route.

visualIdentity.silhouette: sweeping lounge coat with narrow trouser line
visualIdentity.wardrobe: velvet lounge coat, narrow tie, pressed trousers, patent shoes
visualIdentity.hair: slicked dark hair with a silver temple streak
visualIdentity.props: rolled score folio and cigarette case

suspects.portraitPromptBase: resident pianist, sweeping lounge coat with narrow trouser line, patent shoes, ivory piano key cufflink, rolled score folio, cigarette case, deep plum lining accent, easy side-lean posture, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I changed for the mirror call and left before anyone reopened the green room. Any wax in my satchel must be from wardrobe tags, not the ledger drawer.
suspects.profile: Age 31, composed lead understudy balancing ambition, rehearsal politics, and a quietly desperate need for the role promised in the missing ledger notes.
suspects.motive: Needed to remove the dressing ledger before it exposed a secret deal tied to her promotion over the current lead.
suspects.notes: The only suspect with motive, opportunity, and the valid transfer evidence once the red herring and neutral clue are separated correctly.

visualIdentity.silhouette: dramatic rehearsal coat with opened A-line hem
visualIdentity.wardrobe: belted rehearsal coat, slim trousers, heeled boots, stage gloves
visualIdentity.hair: long chestnut waves pinned off one shoulder
visualIdentity.props: structured costume satchel and folded script

suspects.portraitPromptBase: lead understudy, dramatic rehearsal coat with opened A-line hem, heeled boots, crescent brooch, structured costume satchel, folded script, emerald satin scarf accent, poised half-turn stance, full body standing

evidence.title: Brass Cuff Smear
evidence.description: Fresh brass polish on Rowan's cuff appears to match the green room key hook, making him look like the obvious culprit at first glance.
evidence.imagePromptBase: fresh brass polish smear across dark work cuff beside gleaming theatre sconce hardware, close forensic clue, clue communicated only through physical detail

evidence.title: Stage Wax Seam
evidence.description: Green sealing wax from the missing dressing ledger drawer is caught in the inner seam of Elara's costume satchel.
evidence.imagePromptBase: emerald sealing wax caught in stitched leather satchel seam beside drawer splinter, macro physical clue detail

evidence.title: Cue Sheet Pin Mark
evidence.description: A newly pinned cue sheet confirms the mirror-call order changed just before the theft, narrowing when the green room could have been opened.
evidence.imagePromptBase: fresh pin mark beside shifted schedule cards and colored tape tabs on a theatre cue board, contextual backstage clue communicated through arrangement and materials
